Mac and Stella are given the task of identifying the victim, but the evidence points to two periods in Times Square's history, 20 years apart. With Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo teams up with his girlfriend, ATF agent Zoe Keates, to investigate the circumstances surrounding the death of a US Marine in what initially seems like a simple road accident. The team reveals the dead man did indeed die due to a broken neck sustained during a crash, but someone shot at him first. As DiNozzo and Keates pursue their inquiry, they discover the victim's death is connected to a controversial ATF plan to place traceable firearms in the criminal system, and then follow them to the heads of crime organisations. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when a navy intelligence commander responsible for tracking the movements of a Venezuelan politician disappears. programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Tree surgeon Malcolm arrives to deal with the forest's blight problem - and Paul is dismayed to discover the newcomer is Iona's ex-fiance. Molly coaches Jess to compete in the ghillies' gruelling annual challenge, despite the fact the other competitors snub her efforts. Meanwhile, Ewan is envious when Amy starts spending a great deal of time with her art tutor. Highland drama, starring Lloyd Owen and Rae Hendrie

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Beth gets a lead on a drug dealer and volunteers to go undercover to collect more evidence. Max reluctantly agrees and the plucky PC finds herself at a party where she strikes up a friendship with DJ Lenny Jones (David Ajala) but has to leave in a hurry when she spots a man holding a gun. An arrest follows, but loose ends mean she must play her role once more, putting herself into even more danger

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: It is Christmas Day, but Connie fails to feel the festive cheer as her father is rushed in for emergency surgery. Meanwhile, a rare opportunity for a heart-and-lung transplant patient is thrown into jeopardy, and Faye is forced to make the most difficult decision of her life after Jac thwarts her plot to leave England with baby Harry

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Selena considers resigning as police surgeon after failing to section an unstable woman who goes on to endanger the lives of those around her. Charlie distributes leaflets in the red-light district to help the failing hepatitis B clinic, but is arrested after being set up by a disapproving pimp

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ben discovers the body of drug addict Abbey Ferguson and the trail of evidence leads back to her pimp Danny Peters, who witnesses claim lashed out when she refused to sleep with a client who was known to be violent. Stevie and Stuart set out to arrest the brothel keeper, but the encounter takes a horrifying turn when the suspect brandishes a dirty heroin syringe

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Lorraine learns a shocking secret about Grants paternity of Tiffany's baby. Simon agrees to let Dan stay. Peggy comes home from hospital

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy returns from hospital after her cancer operation. Sanjay hangs mistletoe on the stall but takes it down when Gita kisses the male customers

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: An old flame from Northern Ireland turns up on Sergeant McLeod's doorstep with his six-year-old son in tow - a development his wife is not too pleased about. Jeremy has an embarrassing date with Melanie, and soon realises he has more in common with her mother - but she is married to his commanding officer

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Christmas in Skeldale brings no respite from animal emergencies, as pampered pooch Tricky Woo falls ill and Mrs Pumphrey entrusts him to Helen's care. Christopher Timothy and Carol Drinkwater star

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A dawn raid by bailiffs leaves the antiques dealer staring ruin in the face, and with nothing left to look forward to, he decides to turn his back on society. With Ian McShane and guest star Alexei Sayle

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: William organises a special getaway to mark their 24th wedding anniversary. He surprises Hester by taking her back to the hotel where he first proposed - but an even bigger surprise is waiting for them when they get there

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: The staff at Bayview Retirement Village are sent into a panic when Diana and Tom head off to Brighton for the day in a borrowed Porshe without telling anyone. Comedy, starring Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Desperate to earn some cash, Compo reveals a musical talent after being endorsed by several artists in the area and sets out as a one-man band. Comedy, starring Bill Owen, Brian Wilde and Peter Sallis

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy finds an interest in camcorders, much to the annoyance of some country ramblers. When later playing his footage back on Pearl's video machine, it becomes apparent the lens has inadvertently caught her and Howard in a compromising situation. Brian Wilde stars

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When the body of a woman is found, all evidence points to suicide - but Havers is adamant foul play has occurred. Reinstated to the rank of Detective Sergeant and paired once more with Lynley, she sets out to uncover the mysteries surrounding the victim. The trail leads the pair to an exclusive club where Havers goes undercover to delve beneath the respectable surface and find the killer. Jenny Agutter guest stars, with Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Back in 1983, 16-year-old Amy Taskerland was murdered on the night of the school disco. More than 30 years later, the unearthing of a time capsule and the discovery of her voice on a mix tape inside prompts Miller and co to delve back into the case, starting by interviewing Amy's best friend Harriet and her former teacher Mr Hines. The recording features a strange mix of music and poetry, including words from a speech written in secret for the Queen for broadcast in the event of a nuclear war. But how did Amy get a copy of it, and was its secret nature the reason she was killed? Elsewhere, Miller enjoys her first romance since her divorce, while Gerry goes on his future son-in-law's stag night. Amy Nuttall (Emmerdale, Downton Abbey) and Jack Shepherd (Wycliffe) guest star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki and Jack are called to a petrol station where three people have been shot dead, and conclude it is the work of a concealed shooter working from long range - a sniper is at large in London. They combine ballistics and wound pathology with the killer-profiling skills of senior investigator DCI Jane De Freitas, as they work to find this seemingly random murderer before panic hits the city. Starring Emilia Fox, David Caves and Richard Lintern, with Zoe Telford and Steve Wall

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ben discovers the body of drug addict Abbey Ferguson and the trail of evidence leads back to her pimp Danny Peters, who witnesses claim lashed out when she refused to sleep with a client who was known to be violent. Stevie and Stuart set out to arrest the brothel keeper, but the encounter takes a horrifying turn when the suspect brandishes a dirty heroin syringe

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: When the medieval monk investigates the murder of a wealthy cleric, the finger of suspicion immediately points to a new arrival at the abbey, but as Cadfael digs deeper, he uncovers connections to a family with tangled loyalties. Drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Terence Hardiman, Michael Culver, Ian McNeice, Christien Anholt and Julian Glover

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen

Iona prepares to marry Malcolm - who asks his love rival to give the bride away - Molly seeks advice from Hector's spirit, and Donald is freed from an old folks' home thanks to Ewan and Amy's daring escape plan

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: New registrar Antoine Malick makes his mark on the Acute Assessments Unit, and his brash attitude meets with disapproval from Michael and Penny. Meanwhile, Kieran receives a mystery phone call that threatens to derail his budding romance with Donna, and the humiliation of facing a disciplinary hearing prompts Chrissie to wonder whether she should remain at Holby - but it appears the final decision might not be hers to make

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A panic-stricken Kelsey allows a patient to discharge herself, fearing she will spill the beans about their shared past. However, Luke becomes privy to her secret when the woman's condition is discovered to be life-threatening. Sam struggles to cope with the demands of the first day in his new job

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Stuart faces an anxious wait for the HIV test results after being stabbed in the neck with a dirty needle by Danny Peters. Jack is taken aback when the DS turns up for work as usual the next day - not least because he has struck a deal with Peters for inside information on a major heroin smuggling operation, and Stevie has volunteered to work underground in a high-risk sting

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy tells Ian to make an effort over Christmas. Bianca, Ricky and Kathy go on holiday. Martin is rude to Pauline. George proposes to Peggy and she accepts

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Billie's missing Alan and wants him there for Christmas Day. There's an awkward atmosphere between Lorraine and Grant. Pauline and Ruth compete in pleasing Mark

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: As the regiment prepares to leave for the South Atlantic to defend Deliverance Island, Andy contemplates proposing, and Sarah and Jeremy's relationship begins to crumble

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The vets have to contend with an outbreak of illness on the grouse moors, and James tries to save a cow from slaughter. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison star

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The dealer recruits con artists Matron Jackie, Gideon and Henry the Hearse Chaser to attend an auction where he is selling a painting, to purposely bid against each other to bump up the price. However, Lady Jane also bids and after discovering the ruse concocts a plan to get her own back and wipe the smile off Lovejoy's face. Charlotte Cornwell guest stars with Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: William believes Hester's fencing instructor has developed romantic feelings for her. Despite his being a known Lothario, Hester sees no cause for alarm, but is forced to reassess that opinion when inadvertently dropping her guard. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom becomes depressed after suffering a mild heart attack, so the management enlists Diana's help to try to cheer him up. Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: When Auntie Wainwright forces a gigantic tractor wheel on the trio, Foggy decides to craft a vehicle out of it. However, in doing so he manages to prove that the invention of the wheel might not have been such a good idea after all. Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Brian Wilde star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo decides he wants to be up to the physical standard of Rudolph Valentino so Foggy devises an assault course, but Clegg stays out of it

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Jo is shocked when a single mother, fighting a lawsuit against the mobile phone company she blames for her incurable cancer, asks her to take care of her son after she dies. Presiding in the case, Deed finds himself at the centre of a plot between the phone company and Sir Ian Rochester, both of whom are eager to have him out of the way

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gerry is spooked by the discovery of UCOS's latest victim - a cop, whose body has lain untouched under a basement for 30 years. As accusations of police corruption and underhand dealings begin to surface, Gerry has to face up to some skeletons in his own closet and it's a race against time for the team to help before his murky past catches up with him. While babysitting his grandson, Gerry becomes convinced that only he holds the key to the investigation and goes Awol to uncover the truth. Starring Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Double bill. Nikki and Jack investigate when a man is run down and killed by a Tube train - although not long after, a female vicar is murdered on the Underground, leaving them with the difficult prospect of building a case against her distraught husband. However, when a young roofer is stalked and brutally killed on his journey home, CCTV cameras pick up a suspect who links the deaths, and a full-scale manhunt is launched. Meanwhile, Nikki forms a bond with one of the detectives, who asks her to help re-evaluate his father's murder when he was a child. Emilia Fox, David Caves and Richard Lintern star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Stuart faces an anxious wait for the HIV test results after being stabbed in the neck with a dirty needle by Danny Peters. Jack is taken aback when the DS turns up for work as usual the next day - not least because he has struck a deal with Peters for inside information on a major heroin smuggling operation, and Stevie has volunteered to work underground in a high-risk sting

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The monks cause a storm of resentment by launching an expedition to a Welsh village to recover the bones of St Winifred and return them to the abbey. As if that does not make them unpopular enough, a local landowner dies in mysterious circumstances and it is up to Brother Cadfael to clear his colleagues of murder and ensure they get out of the country alive. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi, Michael Culver, John Hallam and Anna Friel

programme.desc: Sisters Sharon and Tracey's lives are changed for ever when their husbands leave for work and never return. First episode of the popular comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The sisters face up to the realities of life with their husbands in prison, and try to explain things to Tracey's son, Garth. Classic comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Stuart faces an anxious wait for the HIV test results after being stabbed in the neck with a dirty needle by Danny Peters. programme.desc: Joseph decides to cut back on his hours at work to spend more time with his infant son, but when Hanssen offers him a promotion, he is forced to make a difficult decision. Meanwhile, Sacha's efforts to make things up with Chrissie end up placing the health of his patients at risk, and when Frieda discovers she is next in line for redundancy, she considers applying for the F1 position

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A distraught Ellen is dealt a double blow when she goes for her scan. Meanwhile, Selena feels frustrated in her relationship with Nathan and contemplates calling it a day

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part story, guest starring Edward Woodward, his son Tim and grandson Sam as three generations of an East End criminal dynasty. programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany has stayed at Simon and Tony's. Peggy gives Grant a lecture about his marriage. Phil says if Grant's marriage is to work he can't always be looking for something better

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy wants Grant to improve the state of his marriage. Lorraine struggles with her feelings about Joe, and Kathy lets Phil know about her New Year plans

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: A remote British-ruled island in the South Pacific is invaded and the King's Own Fusiliers find themselves part of the task force sent to retake it. Unfortunately, Corporal Hobbs is soon captured and tortured by enemy troops, and Colonel Eastwood's daring plan of attack has explosive consequences

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: As part of an economy drive, Siegfried limits the use of an expensive brand of tablets, and Helen adopts a stray cat. The vets introduce new drugs for treating their clients' animals, but have to charge realistic prices for the services rendered. With Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A virtuoso violinist baffles Lovejoy by asking him to help pull off an elaborate con involving a fake Stradivarius - so he enlists an expert to find out what is going on. Whimsical drama, starring Ian McShane

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester ropes in the family to help her organise a church fete. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: The managers of Bayview Retirement Village threaten to expel Diana, prompting Tom to come up with a blackmail plot. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Auntie Wainwright gives the boys a gas grill, but they have difficulty working out how the pressure valve works, bringing an explosive touch to their barbecue. Howard is busy making improvements on his love-nest, but whether it will ignite Marina's passions or just inflame her remains to be seen. Peter Sallis, Bill Owen, Brian Wilde and Robert Fyfe star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo and Foggy drink too much home brew at Ernie Mordue's funeral, resulting in bad hangovers for both of them. Bill Owen and Brian Wilde star

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: A woman is released from prison after a 35-year campaign to clear her of murder - which is bad news for Dalziel, who was the investigating officer responsible for her conviction all those years ago. With his reputation at stake, the bluff copper decides to reinvestigate - and soon uncovers a link with the secret services. Feature-length drama, with guest appearances by Shirley Anne Field, Leslie Phillips and Tom Bell alongside Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Danny takes Gerry on the run as they search for the proof they need to prove his innocence - despite Gerry remaining tight-lipped about his involvement with dodgy dealings in his old unit. The stakes are high as the Unsolved Crime and Open Case Squad goes after one of the biggest crime families in London, and it's those closest to Gerry who are dangerously put in the firing line. Starring Dennis Waterman, Nicholas Lyndhurst, Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Double bill. A suspected paedophile is found dead in a playground, so inexperienced detective Rory Drennan calls in Nikki and Jack to attend. It's not long before the team links his murder to the case of a missing young girl, so the child-protection officers are also brought in - though it soon becomes apparent the youngster's social worker blames herself for the disappearance. Starring Emilia Fox, David Caves and Richard Lintern, with Claudie Blakley, Lorraine Burroughs and John Dagliesh

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part story, guest starring Edward Woodward, his son Tim and grandson Sam as three generations of an East End criminal dynasty. Ageing gangster Johnnie Jackson, sensing the onset of Alzheimer's disease, tries to rescue his grandson from a life of crime, but the seeds of destruction are already sown as young Jacko finds himself working for ruthless drug dealer Roger Hutton

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The widow of a rich merchant stirs up a tempest of lust as the men of Shrewsbury vie for her hand in marriage - and it seems one has no qualms about murdering all his rivals. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi, Terrence Hardiman, Michael Culver and Julian Firth

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a young family to escape the city and find some space and tranquillity in the Hampshire countryside with their £800,000 budget

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Good friends Heather and Kelly provide today's ingredients for Akis Petretzikis and Romy Gill, hoping the chefs will give them some ideas on dishes they can recreate at home. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The restorers work on a pre-war metal pedal plane that has been grounded for decades, a ukulele fashioned from an old cigar box and a collection of badly broken toy zoo animals

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Labour Party candidate for the London mayoral election

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon gives Jean a chance to talk through her family concerns, although when Sharon mentions her new-found brother, Jean is left with an idea. Martin is caught in the middle as Lily and Ruby argue, and Whitney realises Kush is worried about leaving Arthur. Isaac reminds his mother Sheree of her controlling tendencies as she refuses to allow Patrick take part in the medical trial

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The truth is out about Reyhan's abuse, although Sahira is adamant that her father is innocent, leaving Hanssen feeling compromised. Louis discovers old friend Vicky is caught in a toxic relationship and vows to help. But who can he turn to? Kian tries to convince Lucky that his drug taking is a thing of the past

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Nurse Chloe starts her first shift at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, while a toddler arrives having difficulty breathing

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: Keeley and the syndicate tell Mercury Millions they are the real lottery winners and that Frank has stolen their money. Without their ticket, however, they have no proof - so they realise their only option is to track him down in Monaco themselves. As they scrape together the money for their flights, Frank is living the high life - but his outrageous gambling is attracting attention all over the city. Meanwhile, a customer asks Colette out for a drink, leaving her worried that if she gets close to him, he might learn the truth about her past

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary following how British Muslims mark some of life's biggest moments. Married couple She and Ali look back at their vows and reflect on how their faith has helped them in their marriage. Emine marks the death of her mother with a final prayer, symbolising her mum coming from god, and going back to god

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Roman Catholics Maria and Peter have their fifth child baptised in a traditional Latin service and in Stockton-on-Tees family and friends pay their last respects to June, one of the first women to be ordained as a priest by the Church of the England in 1994

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma's brother Caleb puts in an appearance, and she gets an ally in her campaign to prevent the bypass. Meanwhile, a girl captures Norman's interest. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga, Freddie Highmore and Max Thieriot

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Dylan wonders where his loyalties lie and Norma is initiated into society by Christine. Meanwhile, Norman is less than happy about having Caleb around. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga, Freddie Highmore and Max Thieriot

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in the Kent seaside town of Margate, the Lancashire town of Carnforth, and a residence near Chesterfield

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: The burglars targeting high-value properties, including the home of a Premier League footballer - and how they were caught thanks to a helicopter with heat-seeking cameras

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Just outside Edinburgh, paramedics Dan and Leigh rush to a car park following reports of a patient having a seizure, and an Oban crew help a regular patient struggling to breathe

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw and Caroline Hawley help two teams look for items at Newark Antiques Fair in Nottinghamshire, before seeing if they make profits at auction. Anita Manning presents

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Altrincham hoping to find some hidden gems she can transform and send off to a new home. Bex Simon almost meets her match with a filing cabinet that requires all her creative flair and upholstery artist Ray Clarke struggles to coax an old sofa back to life

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple with a £900,000 budget find a home in the Worcestershire countryside, so they can be closer to loved ones. Jonnie also visits Droitwich Spa

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Competitive couple Declan and Clare take on the culinary challenge, keen to step outside their comfort zone and try a few new ingredients with the help of chefs Jeremy Pang and Ellis Barrie. Presented by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Master hatter Jayesh Vaghela is brought to the barn to tackle a treasured hat worn by a PoW who worked on the Death Railway in the Second World War. Mechanical expert Steve Fletcher is flummoxed by a talking doll who has lost her voice and silversmith Brenton West takes on the repair of a musical salt and pepper set of great sentimental value to owner Janice

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Conservative Party candidate for London mayor

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Special episode celebrating the life of the motor racing driver and Top Gear presenter. Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris, Paddy McGuinness are joiined by former hosts Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond, James May, Matt LeBlanc and Rory Reid and well-known figures from the world of motoring to share their fondest memories of the Queen of the Nurburgring

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring another four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Electronics wizard Mark Stuckey works on a home-made gramophone from the late 1880s that has lost its voice - and its horn. Mechanic expert Steve Fletcher restores a set of grocer's scales, Suzie Fletcher works on a leather keepsake bag and woodwork expert Will Kirk tackles one of his most intricate repairs yet - an ornamental Egyptian table whose ornate carvings have been crushed to pieces

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love and Jamali Maddix are joined by guest panellist Jimmy Carr to identify the real Gemma from a line-up of four, all claiming to own the same house in Farnham, Surrey. The house may be easy to spot with its colourful cladding and bright interior, but the real Gemma is not. One says she has gone to town with the decor after becoming newly single, another claims she decorates her house to be 'liked' on social media, the third is a die-hard rainbow fan and the fourth is out to impress to her neighbours. Hosted by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: When Nudes Are Stolen
programme.desc: Former glamour model Jess Davies stopped doing topless shots years ago. Yet since then she has received hundred, if not thousands, of messages from men telling her they have been speaking to someone using her pictures. Here she sets out to find out about the online trade in nude and topless photos, exploring the broader culture in which people have no concerns about sharing and trading people's intimate images and give little consideration to whether the pictures were taken consensually

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam sees first-hand how the sports worlds is coping during the pandemic by following the build-up to a YouTube fight taking place behind closed doors and streamed live to an audience of millions. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams, Tim Key and Eleanor Nawal

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam Williams must help a cafe appeal to younger diners, so he visits food photographer Declan for a tutorial on taking better pictures of meals. He also gathers iinspiration from a restaurant that's a favourite among Instagrammers. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey Solomon challenges members of the Yaku family to declutter their home in seven days. In order to do this, they have to let go of half their possessions so that they can then reorganise their property. Emotional moments lie in store as the family goes through its most treasured items and friction develops as members decide what - and what not - to part with. Mum Tash in particular struggles to let go of the hundreds of sentimental items that took up much needed space. Plus, tidying and upcycling tips from home improvement gurus Dilly Carter, Iwan Carrington and Robert Bent

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The renovation of a semi-detached in Bilston, West Midlands, and a mid-terrace in Chatham, Kent, as well as a derelict Welsh bungalow that turned into a four-year project

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Rav Wilding recounts the moment armed robbers burst into a jewellers' in Hinkley, Leicestershire. Even though they wore masks and gloves to hide their identities, they left behind a single footprint

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: John and Suzanne race to the centre of Glasgow to help a homeless patient with a head injury. In Edinburgh, Moira and Dee struggle through the city centre traffic to assist a woman who has fallen out of her wheelchair, and use a clever piece of kit to help her off the ground

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross is in charge of two teams of bargain bunters at Nottingham racecourse. Expert advice comes from Thomas Plant and Nick Hall. Meanwhile, Charlie test the fairgoers' knowledge of hallmarks

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore looks round Earlswood Recycling Centre, trying to find rubbish she can rescue and revamp. Sarah enlists the help of her trusty transformers to take on some tricky items. Mark Haig has an electrical meter to take on, while wood wizards Josh and Oli are faced with a dated table. Sarah herself turns her hands to a vintage rocking horse

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah embarks on a hunt for a rural home in the Derbyshire Peak District with a couple of soon-to-be retirees who have a budget of £500,000, and for whom a pond is a high priority. Away from the house hunt, Sonali gets battle ready when she meets a master armourer and his apprentice who are upholding the tradition of this metalwork craft

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Sisters Liz and Maddie both love to cook and here wish to add some brand-new dishes to their repertoire. Liz is in the Green Pepper kitchen spicing things up with chef Romy Gill and Maddie is in the Red Pepper kitchen bringing out maximum flavour with chef Anna Haugh. Presented by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Antique camera restorer Brenton West tackles a vintage pocket camera, believed to have taken the final photo of a loving family before they dispersed. Amanda Middleditch and Julie Tatchell get to grips with a stuffed pig that is crying out for attention, while ceramics expert Kirsten Ramsay painstakingly pieces back together a delicate stone plate from India

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Green Party candidate for the London mayoral election

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan host the nightly magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Despite Sharon telling new-found brother Zack to leave, he buys her breakfast - but when her back is turned, he swipes her keys. Kat wants her fling with Phil kept quiet, although after overhearing him booking a romantic holiday, she is secretly overjoyed. Kush visits Ruby about Arthur but she knows he has only plucked up the courage to speak to her because Martin is out. Bailey admits she won the science competition to Mitch, who assures her they'll find the money to attend the camp

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A pair of university graduates hope that their organic superfood powders will supercharge the Dragons' pockets. A low-calorie craft lager business brews up trouble over its high valuation, a dental-minded duo demonstrate their plaque-detecting app, while there's a heartfelt pitch from an entrepreneur hoping to up-scale her range of afro and curly hair products. Whether Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani, Sara Davies or Touker Suleyman will be prompted to invest in all - or any - of the products being pitched remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The final four amateur cooks interpret a classic dish of their choice before the best three face a Chef's Table challenge at Michel Roux Jr's acclaimed restaurant Le Gavroche, As usual, John Torode and Gregg Wallace assess the participants' skills to a bid to assess that they can be worthy finalists, joined in their endeavours by Monica Galetti, Steve Groves and Michel's daughter Emily Roux

programme.title: Avengers Assemble (2012)
programme.desc: A mystical artefact is stolen from a top-secret military base by Thor's brother Loki, who plans to use it to bring about an alien invasion. Spymaster Nick Fury brings together a team of superheroes, including Iron Man, Thor, Captain America and the Hulk, to defend Earth from the impending attack. Action adventure uniting the Marvel comic-book characters, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Evans, Chris Hemsworth, Mark Ruffalo, Scarlett Johansson, Tom Hiddleston, Jeremy Renner and Samuel L Jackson

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: An elderly woman has her handbag snatched on her way to bingo - but the robber doesn't get far. Police follow a digital trail of evidence and put him behind bars

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don makes the most of the clocks going forward as he begins to sow dahlia seeds for summer flowers. He also reveals a new project, and shows what to plant now in the vegetable garden. Adam Frost extends the planting in his gravel garden and reveals his plans for the coming year

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which a shipping company manager, a teacher and a singer must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold presents highlights and analysis from the second round of matches in the Super League season, including St Helens v Hull Kingston Rovers, Wigan Warriors v Wakefield Trinity, Hull FC v Salford Red Devils and Leeds Rhinos v Castleford Tigers

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: With George and Smiler along for the ride, Woody makes his secret trip to Australia. However, there is a shocking surprise in store there for him. Back in Weld, Shay remains the object of Ollie's unwanted affections. Comedy drama, with Rick Donald

programme.title: Nature's Great Migrations
programme.desc: Documentary following a thousand elephants as they come together in a crucial gathering in northern Kenya, while facing threats from both predators and poachers. Liz Bonnin and a team of scientists and filmmakers use cutting-edge tracking technology to follow individual elephants as they cope with daily challenges and make the most of this unique, short-lived gathering. They also capture surprising new behaviour and witness how the elephant society responds to poaching, including the way in which young elephants cope with the loss of their parents

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: The documentary series exploring some of the world's most famous ecosystems continues, this time focusing on the Serengeti, the east African grassland that has become home to some of the greatest concentrations of herbivores on the continent. Steve Backshall explains how this landscape developed into such a diverse and densely populated habitat

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team value antiques at Birmingham's Museum and Art Gallery, where experts Thomas Plant and Christina Trevanion examine items brought in by members of the public. Meanwhile, Paul Martin visits Hagley Hall in Worcestershire, the last great Palladian house to be built in Britain

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya, Hal Cruttenden, Kerry Godliman and Ebony Rainford-Brent go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: A beloved gramophone, which reminds the owner of her music-loving grandad, and a old slide projector belonging to a photography enthusiast are among today's transformations

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: A couple living in a Bungalow near Brighton are treated to two virtual reality visions for what their home could become by architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison. Silvia and Julian bought the property because they loved the garden, and thought they could make something of the house - but their renovations have not been a success. Robert proposes a way to make the planning rules that have restricted them work for the project, while Laura finds a way to bring the beloved garden inside the house. Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Another host of leading food experts take Fred Sirieix to their favourite restaurants to explore what makes them so special. In this edition, Chris Bavin takes Fred on a culinary venture around the New Forest, where fresh local produce is king, featuring wood-fired cooking, pork pies and a country pub serving a special Ploughman's

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti explore one of the world's most luxurious eco resorts on the small island of Tetiaroa in French Polynesia. Surrounded by a coral reef and an abundance of marine life, they work alongside staff of the exclusive Brando Resort to serve five-star luxury to guests paying up to £11,000 per a night

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Man Like Mobeen star Guz Khan hosts the stand-up showcase recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. On the bill are Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Rhys James

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Pandemic 2020
programme.desc: The story of the first year of the pandemic told by people from all over the world. It begins on New Year's Eve 2019, when huge crowds gathered to celebrate. Contributors include Xie, a young food blogger from Wuhan whose wedding took place a few days before her city went into lockdown. As the virus travels to Europe, Dr Amie from Coventry recalls watching in disbelief the horrific scenes of people dying in Italy

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team takes some of greatest-ever cars featured in James Bond movies for a spin, including the iconic Aston Martin DB5. Freddie Flintoff gets behind the wheel of an Extreme E race car and puts it to the test against a man in a jet-pack, while Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness test a new Ferrari against a very old Alfa

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace present the last of the heats which sees five more hopefuls competing for an apron by cooking their signature dish - something that tells John and Gregg about who they are as a cook and how good they can become. The two best will automatically go through, while the remaining three must cook two courses for John and Gregg as well as Dhruv Baker and Alex Rushmer - champion and finalist from the 2010 competition

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett travels to the Blackdown Hills on the Somerset-Devon border, where he turns a 1960s trailer into a funky festival-themed accommodation

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: The search for ram raiders who targeted a jewellers in Northampton - and how automatic number plate recognition cameras eventually saw them put them behind bars

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Yvonne and Julian learn about the impact on CODAs or children of deaf adults, the launch of the Green Room podcast and the importance of having a positive body image. With voiceover

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and team head to the Forest of Dean, where they help Jon and Michelle to turn their old horse cart into a cosy and comfortable sanctuary for guests

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants Michael, Laura and Dubem must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three terraces in Cardiff are in the spotlight, featuring an Edwardian property with a modern update, one designed with a nod to the peaceful, and a revamped Victorian house

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George's house is even more full of waifs and strays than usual, and the first step to him having some peace and quiet is to get Tracy to forgive Woody

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents a look back at the Major from the Augusta National in Georgia, which Tiger Woods entered as the defending champion. With commentary from Peter Alliss

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores the ecosystem of the Amazon rainforest, offering an insight into a world of intricate relationships and unexpected hardship. He also reveals a hidden secret that may be the key to keeping the jungle's inhabitants alive

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From Wallasey Town Hall on the Wirral Peninsula, where antiques experts Philip Serrell and Nick Davies select the best items to take to auction. Paul Martin gets special permission to go behind the scenes with National Museums Liverpool, to find out how they preserve the millions of often priceless objects that are in their care, at the Conservation Studios in the city centre

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya, Hal Cruttenden, Kerry Godliman and Ebony Rainford-Brent test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: A huge wooden playhouse and some traditional Jamaican fabric that connects a mother and daughter six thousand miles apart are among today's transformations

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison use virtual reality to redesign a 1930s Surrey house with a poorly designed layout to make better use of the space, and allow the owners to both start a family and have their parents move in with them in the future. Laura's plan involves turning the property into a light-filled interconnected series of spaces, while Robert turns conventional wisdom on its head with a visually striking and flexible design. After experiencing both potential houses, the owners must choose which they would like to put into practice

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four top chefs compete for the London and the South East region with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. One of the four contestants must leave the competition at the end of the episode, as decided by this week's veteran judge

programme.title: The Terror
programme.desc: The expedition reaches its climax as the men find themselves in a final confrontation with the mythological entities whose land they have trespassed into. An injured Crozier is brought to Hickey's camp, leading to a final reconciliation between them, while Edward Little tries to organise a rescue party in the face of objections from his crew

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the capital city of Denmark, Copenhagen, where he is inspired to cook fried pork belly with lovage potatoes and parsley sauce - a popular Danish dish

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Johnny Vegas, Ronni Ancona and Jimmy Carr join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'naval navigation' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Finding Jack Charlton
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke presents a profile of the World Cup-winning footballer, looking at his special relationship with Ireland after becoming the national team's manager, his complicated relationship with brother Bobby, and his final battle with dementia. including contributions from his wife Pat and son John, alongside celebrated Irish players, musicians, writers and politicians, including Larry Mullen, Roddy Doyle, Brendan O'Carroll, Paul McGrath, Niall Quinn, Mick McCarthy, David O'Leary and Andy Townsend

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Yvonne and Julian learn about the impact on CODAs, the launch of the Green Room podcast and the importance of having a positive body image. With voiceover

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Having survived seven tough design challenges, the two finalists go head-to-head to battle it out for the ultimate prize as they are challenged to transform luxury holiday villas in Cornwall, before Judge Michelle Ogundehin decides who will be this year's winner. Presented by Alan Carr

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last heat sees the final five amateurs cook their Signature Dish - something that tells judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace about their potential, as they pick their two favourites with those cooks earning a place in the next round. The remaining three have to pull out all the stops as they cook-off in a new test - the Favourite Ingredient Challenge. After which, those remaining must cook two courses that excite not just John and Gregg but also to former champions Thomasina Miers and Tim Anderson, as they vie for a quarter-final place and the chance to be MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: The burglars targeting high-value properties, including the home of a Premier League footballer - and how they were caught thanks to a helicopter with heat-seeking cameras

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: Chris Packham shines a light on some of the species that show how brainpower, ingenuity and clever tactics are just as important as brawn in the daily battle for survival. Spotted hyenas play politics to gain power, and the female with the most friends rules their group, while being a team player can help when it comes to getting a meal - as demonstrated by vampire bats, who share mouthfuls of blood with strangers in the hope of building a bond and receiving the favour in return

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Competing in Cardiff for a place in the final are a renovated, three-storey semi-detached, a period semi with a modern twist and a rustic, shabby-chic home with a love of upcycling

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: With the wedding back on, it's time for Woody's 'groomspiel', and Big Mac has wild plans for it. However, Woody has made Tracy a promise he'll behave - which is going to be hard to keep. Meanwhile, new arrival Poppy and Arlo are getting on so well that Lindsay is jealous, even if Arlo is too innocent to realise

programme.title: Nature's Great Migrations
programme.desc: Documentary following 100,000 caribou as they face bears, wolves, frozen rivers and rugged mountains to undertake the world's longest land migration. Liz Bonnin and a team of scientists track the herd as they travel 5,000km through the Arctic wilderness of Canada and Alaska, revealing insights into the lives of individual animals as they attempt the epic journey. Cameras capture the caribou as they fight to reach their calving grounds before they give birth - failure would spell disaster

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores Monterey Bay, which lies just along the coast of California. He reveals how the unique geography and delicate balance of marine life, from microscopic plankton to visiting ocean giants, provide the key to its thriving ecosystem

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of finds from various locations around the country, including Lulworth Castle in Dorset, Chiddingstone Castle in Kent and Wrest Park, Bedfordshire

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya, Hal Cruttenden, Kerry Godliman and Ebony Rainford-Brent test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: Transformations include a special recumbent tricycle that once carried an Olympic torch, and a widow's wish for her wedding and engagement rings comes true. Sabrina Grant hosts

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison come up with virtual reality designs to transform a Halifax couple's 19th-century home. When owner Paula developed MS, it became vital to make the house more wheelchair accessible - but her partner Jeremy also sees an opportunity to give it a stylish new look. Laura's concept makes the most of the views of the garden and uses clever recycling tricks to stretch the budget, while Robert deploys off-the-shelf materials to making the place more accessible

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Once again, they each serve a unique take on the brief celebrating British invention and innovation. Whose inventions in the kitchen will veteran judge Paul Ainsworth consider the best? Only two will proceed to Friday's judging and have a chance to represent the London and the South East region at the regional finals. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Pandemic 2020
programme.desc: Told through candid interviews and personal archive, cameras reveal what happens as lockdowns continue around the planet and the pressure of being trapped indoors begins to be felt. Contributors include a French photojournalist who is forced to decide between working and home-schooling, and heads to the frontline of the pandemic

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians Tom Allen, Angela Barnes, Rhys James, Nigel Ng and Athena Kugblenu. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels to the icy summit of Britain's highest and most wintery mountain, Ben Nevis, to discover how Scotland was first promoted as a winter holiday destination

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories, with Emma Barnett

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at a celebrity's life through 10 defining photos, with the star's secrets revealed by those who were there, and those who knew them best. The first edition focuses on Queen frontman Freddie Mercury, beginning with the earliest photo of the singer as a baby, and ending with a private moment captured shortly before his death

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the first day of the Major, taking place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia, where Dustin Johnson prevailed last year. With commentary from Andrew Cotter, Ken Brown, Iona Stephen, Mark James and Iain Carter

programme.title: Football's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: Three-part documentary exploring historic child abuse that took place in youth football in England from the 1970s to the 1990s and the code of silence surrounding it. The first episode focuses on former footballer Andy Woodward's 2016 revelation of abuse at the hands of a youth coach, and the personal accounts of players from various youth sides

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last quarter-final sees the week's quartet of talented heat-winners return to fight for a semi-final place by facing the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by celebrated food critic Grace Dent - is to cook a dish inspired by global street food. Presented by John Torode and Gregg Wallace

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team travel to Hampshire to meet Kerie and Dave, to transform an abandoned shepherd's hut and pigsty into a unique B&B

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming gets stuck in the mud in Woodseaves Cutting, and explores the charming canal-side village of Kinver in Staffordshire

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross captures a warm winter morning and the freshness of a new day still not quite awake, with its surroundings undisturbed by nature's little creatures

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Jim Hacker supports a departmental construction project, but Sir Humphrey's evasiveness and financial difficulties force the minister into a deal with a union leader. Starring Paul Eddington, Nigel Hawthorne and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: The Fall and Rise of Reginald Perrin
programme.desc: Reggie attends his own memorial service, and is disturbed to see how close his grieving widow has become to Henry Possett. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and Pauline Yates

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The stories of murder investigations and their extraordinary consequences, which overturned laws, transformed police interrogation and revolutionised forensic detection. The second episode focuses on the story of the murder of Julie Hogg, a single mother from the small town of Billingham in Teesside. The programme looks back at Julie's mother Ann's campaign to challenge the 800-year-old law on Double Jeopardy, after a jury failed to convict Julie's former boyfriend Billy Dunlop. As this episode traces the twists and turns of Ann's campaign, the story of other cases that benefited from the legal changes are also explored

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary examining the history of capital punishment, including the protocol of public execution and the science of hanging. Archive information reveals the offences that resulted in the gallows and how children as young as seven were hanged

programme.title: Lights Up Festival
programme.desc: Jazmin is the only mixed-race woman in her small west country village and suffers racism on a regular basis. She longs to break free of the bullies and move to the big city to become an actress. Semi-autobiographical drama, written by and starring Natasha Marshall

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary looking at the reopening of a 1974 murder case, when new evidence led to the exposure one of the most shocking miscarriages of justice Europe has ever witnessed

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: In the second programme, the historian explores how science and detection influenced the popular culture of murder during Victorian times. Writers including Charles Dickens and Wilkie Collins were fascinated by grisly crimes, and the literary genre that came out of it captured the imagination of readers. The presenter also reveals that when Jack the Ripper began his reign of terror in London at the same time Robert Louis Stevenson's Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde was published, the idea of the serial killer was introduced to the British public

programme.title: Dark Son: The Hunt for a Serial Killer
programme.desc: Criminologist Professor David Wilson examines the killing of six women in 1960s London, a case that remains unsolved. He considers another case from 1921, in which a teenage boy in a Monmouthshire village murdered two schoolgirls, and questions if the treatment of the bodies in both cases suggests they were the work of the same culprit

programme.title: Lights Up Festival
programme.desc: Drama, written by and starring Katherine Parkinson as one of three characters sitting for a silent painter. As Mary, Luke and Cassandra have their pictures painted, they reveal truths about loves, loss and the importance of human connection. Directed by Jeremy Herrin, former artistic director of Headlong Theatre, and co-starring Mark Weinman and Alex Jarrett

programme.title: How to Make
programme.desc: Designer and materials engineer Zoe Laughlin dismantles and dissects three classic items to understand the wonders of form, function and material that go into making them, before building her own truly bespoke versions step by step. She begins with modern classic the training shoe, meeting some of the UK's leading designers and manufacturers, as well as young inventors working on sustainable creations such as material made by bacteria and self-deodorising fabrics

programme.desc: In 1884, ambitious young surgeon Frederick Treves is intrigued by a sideshow freak billed as the Elephant Man. He finds, behind the disfigured exhibit, an intelligent person called John Merrick, whose rehabilitation he undertakes, with the severely deformed man's life being transformed by the friendship that develops between the two. Fact-based drama directed by David Lynch, starring John Hurt, Anthony Hopkins, Anne Bancroft, John Gielgud, Wendy Hiller and Freddie Jones

programme.title: The $50million Art Swindle
programme.desc: Documentary telling the remarkable story of a charlatan art dealer who swindled more than $50million from the art establishment before going on the run. Michel Cohen, a popular and charming New York art dealer originally from France, was a high-school drop-out from a poor background who went on to become a rich and successful art dealer. Throughout the 1990s, he sold paintings by artists such as Picasso, Monet and Chagall to America's wealthiest elite. Cohen was living the high life until he began trading recklessly in the stock market and ran up considerable debts. In an attempt to recoup his losses, he swindled private collectors, auction houses and other art dealers out of more than $50million. When his crimes were discovered, he fled the US with his wife and two children and went on the run. In 2003, he was found by Interpol in Brazil and was imprisoned in Rio de Janeiro, but seven months later while awaiting extradition to the US, he escaped from prison and vanished off the face o

programme.desc: Steve goes on a mission to track down the deadliest of all dinosaur defenders. He meets the armoured tank that is Nodosaurus, tests whether an Iguanodon really has what it takes to beat a T-Rex, and - thanks to some mega engineering - unleashes an Ankylosaurus tail-swinging robot that goes on the rampage

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Plastic is one of the greatest inventions in human history. It makes so much of our lives possible, but how we dispose of it is now a massive problem. By the year 2050 it’s thought that there will be more plastic than fish in our seas. So what can we do about it? First, ditch the disposable plastic water bottle and go for tap water in a reusable bottle. Better for the environment and cheaper too! Next, join a beach clean. It may seem like this problem is too big for us to handle but many hands make light work. Reduce, reuse and, importantly, recycle. And even more importantly than that, don’t drop litter. A bag that is thrown on the streets of London could end up on the coast of Africa or in the belly of a whale or a turtle. Lastly and most importantly, know that every single person can make a difference. One single plastic bag could choke a turtle or sunfish. One plastic straw could be the end of a gorgeous, iconic animal, so know you have power and use it.

programme.desc: Join 11-month-old Rudi and Everley as they discover the world around them and see what they do to relieve their teething pain. Rudi and Everley begin their day eating breakfast with their families. Everley and her family are in the garden, looking at each other’s teeth in a mirror and counting how many they have. Rudi then plays with a teething mitten to soothe his teeth, before having fresh fruit. Everley visits her family dentist, where the dentist checks her teeth and talks to mum about the importance of teething. Once home, Everley’s mum gives Everley her very own toothbrush! programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Zahra-Rose's family is from Somalia, a country situated on the east coast of Africa. In Somalia, they have the traditional dish of nafaqo, which is boiled egg wrapped in mashed potato. This is what Zahra-Rose has chosen to make for her friends. To begin with, Zahra-Rose seasons mashed potato with salt, black pepper, flour, ground cumin, ground coriander, turmeric powder and fresh coriander. She then wraps the potato mixture around her special ingredient, hard-boiled eggs, rolls them in breadcrumbs and puts them in the oven to be cooked. Zahra-Rose also serves a fresh chilli sauce, so her friends can use it for dipping. Not only does Zahra-Rose have beautiful decorations, she also has a surprise picnic planned. Will her friends realise what her special ingredient is?

They are put onto skewers with an oil, oregano, honey and lemon marinade and put into the oven with his pitta bread. Emilio has made a Cypriot wishing tree for his friends to tie their wishes to. Both families are playing at home, talking about colours and a four-legged friend. They all head to baby classes, where they use music to practice their words, and some puppets come along to help them learn! Back at home, Chester and Elias play in the garden with their brother, going up and down the slide. Gia takes mum and granddad for a walk in the woods, where they see lots of beautiful things - they even find a bird! Finally, Chester and Elias play a counting game and Gia makes a wonderful word wall in the garden using pictures of things they saw that day. Remember, every child develops differently.

She starts by adding garlic puree, Korean chilli paste, soy sauce, honey, toasted sesame oil and her special ingredient, sesame seeds, into a jug and mixing them all up. Next, Hannah puts pieces of her salmon onto individual baking sheets and spoons the marinade on. She garnishes them with freshly chopped spring onions, and then her salmon is ready to be cooked in the oven. Hannah wears a colourful Korean outfit and treats her friends to some local Korean kimchi, but will her pals be able to guess her special ingredient?

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook meets four generations of food lovers in Cardiff. While joining the Aslams to rustle up some of their favourite curries, fish dishes and a keema bolognese, Michela meets 90-year-old head of the family Mohammed, who came from Bangladesh in 1958 and went on to open one of Wales' very first Asian restaurants. Michela tries to prise the family's secret spice mix as well as helping grandson Yusuf to master one of his grandmother's specialities to bring along to a special party to celebrate his grandparents' 54th wedding anniversary

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring another four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Electronics wizard Mark Stuckey works on a home-made gramophone from the late 1880s that has lost its voice - and its horn. Mechanic expert Steve Fletcher restores a set of grocer's scales, Suzie Fletcher works on a leather keepsake bag and woodwork expert Will Kirk tackles one of his most intricate repairs yet - an ornamental Egyptian table whose ornate carvings have been crushed to pieces

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love and Jamali Maddix are joined by guest panellist Jimmy Carr to identify the real Gemma from a line-up of four, all claiming to own the same house in Farnham, Surrey. The house may be easy to spot with its colourful cladding and bright interior, but the real Gemma is not. One says she has gone to town with the decor after becoming newly single, another claims she decorates her house to be 'liked' on social media, the third is a die-hard rainbow fan and the fourth is out to impress to her neighbours. Hosted by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: When Nudes Are Stolen
programme.desc: Former glamour model Jess Davies stopped doing topless shots years ago. Yet since then she has received hundred, if not thousands, of messages from men telling her they have been speaking to someone using her pictures. Here she sets out to find out about the online trade in nude and topless photos, exploring the broader culture in which people have no concerns about sharing and trading people's intimate images and give little consideration to whether the pictures were taken consensually

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam sees first-hand how the sports worlds is coping during the pandemic by following the build-up to a YouTube fight taking place behind closed doors and streamed live to an audience of millions. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams, Tim Key and Eleanor Nawal

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey Solomon challenges members of the Yaku family to declutter their home in seven days. In order to do this, they have to let go of half their possessions so that they can then reorganise their property. Emotional moments lie in store as the family goes through its most treasured items and friction develops as members decide what - and what not - to part with. Mum Tash in particular struggles to let go of the hundreds of sentimental items that took up much needed space. Plus, tidying and upcycling tips from home improvement gurus Dilly Carter, Iwan Carrington and Robert Bent

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Father
programme.desc: The emotional story of Jackie Saleh's fight to find her three children after their were kidnapped by their father in 1986 and taken to Yemen. Police discovered that Jackie's Yemeni husband Sadek Saleh had boarded a plane to Saudi Arabia, but the British government were powerless to trace the children. Three decades after their disappearance, her youngest daughter Sofia, who was a toddler when she vanished, is finally returning to Cardiff with her own family, fleeing the conflict in Yemen which saw their house being bombed. The programme follows the mother and daughter as they are reunited for the first time, as they attempt to form a relationship which was taken from them decades ago

programme.title: Avengers Assemble (2012)

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Maggie has flashbacks to Joe's murder, but will they exonerate Caitlin or help convict her? The O'Hara flat is emptied by debt collectors, Cameron plays mind games with Dylan, and Rory is shocked to see who has moved into the Murdoch home.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Special episode celebrating the life of the motor racing driver and Top Gear presenter. Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris, Paddy McGuinness are joiined by former hosts Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond, James May, Matt LeBlanc and Rory Reid and well-known figures from the world of motoring to share their fondest memories of the Queen of the Nurburgring

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring another four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Electronics wizard Mark Stuckey works on a home-made gramophone from the late 1880s that has lost its voice - and its horn. Mechanic expert Steve Fletcher restores a set of grocer's scales, Suzie Fletcher works on a leather keepsake bag and woodwork expert Will Kirk tackles one of his most intricate repairs yet - an ornamental Egyptian table whose ornate carvings have been crushed to pieces

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love and Jamali Maddix are joined by guest panellist Jimmy Carr to identify the real Gemma from a line-up of four, all claiming to own the same house in Farnham, Surrey. The house may be easy to spot with its colourful cladding and bright interior, but the real Gemma is not. One says she has gone to town with the decor after becoming newly single, another claims she decorates her house to be 'liked' on social media, the third is a die-hard rainbow fan and the fourth is out to impress to her neighbours. Hosted by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: When Nudes Are Stolen
programme.desc: Former glamour model Jess Davies stopped doing topless shots years ago. Yet since then she has received hundred, if not thousands, of messages from men telling her they have been speaking to someone using her pictures. Here she sets out to find out about the online trade in nude and topless photos, exploring the broader culture in which people have no concerns about sharing and trading people's intimate images and give little consideration to whether the pictures were taken consensually

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam sees first-hand how the sports worlds is coping during the pandemic by following the build-up to a YouTube fight taking place behind closed doors and streamed live to an audience of millions. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams, Tim Key and Eleanor Nawal

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam Williams must help a cafe appeal to younger diners, so he visits food photographer Declan for a tutorial on taking better pictures of meals. He also gathers iinspiration from a restaurant that's a favourite among Instagrammers. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams
