Here she sets out to find out about the online trade in nude and topless photos, exploring the broader culture in which people have no concerns about sharing and trading people's intimate images and give little consideration to whether the pictures were taken consensually

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam sees first-hand how the sports worlds is coping during the pandemic by following the build-up to a YouTube fight taking place behind closed doors and streamed live to an audience of millions. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams, Tim Key and Eleanor Nawal

programme.title: Pls Like
programme.desc: Liam Williams must help a cafe appeal to younger diners, so he visits food photographer Declan for a tutorial on taking better pictures of meals. He also gathers iinspiration from a restaurant that's a favourite among Instagrammers. Spoof documentary, starring Liam Williams

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey Solomon challenges members of the Yaku family to declutter their home in seven days. In order to do this, they have to let go of half their possessions so that they can then reorganise their property. Emotional moments lie in store as the family goes through its most treasured items and friction develops as members decide what - and what not - to part with. Mum Tash in particular struggles to let go of the hundreds of sentimental items that took up much needed space. Plus, tidying and upcycling tips from home improvement gurus Dilly Carter, Iwan Carrington and Robert Bent

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The renovation of a semi-detached in Bilston, West Midlands, and a mid-terrace in Chatham, Kent, as well as a derelict Welsh bungalow that turned into a four-year project

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Rav Wilding recounts the moment armed robbers burst into a jewellers' in Hinkley, Leicestershire. Even though they wore masks and gloves to hide their identities, they left behind a single footprint

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: John and Suzanne race to the centre of Glasgow to help a homeless patient with a head injury. In Edinburgh, Moira and Dee struggle through the city centre traffic to assist a woman who has fallen out of her wheelchair, and use a clever piece of kit to help her off the ground

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross is in charge of two teams of bargain bunters at Nottingham racecourse. Expert advice comes from Thomas Plant and Nick Hall. Meanwhile, Charlie test the fairgoers' knowledge of hallmarks

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore looks round Earlswood Recycling Centre, trying to find rubbish she can rescue and revamp. Sarah enlists the help of her trusty transformers to take on some tricky items. Mark Haig has an electrical meter to take on, while wood wizards Josh and Oli are faced with a dated table. Sarah herself turns her hands to a vintage rocking horse

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah embarks on a hunt for a rural home in the Derbyshire Peak District with a couple of soon-to-be retirees who have a budget of £500,000, and for whom a pond is a high priority. Away from the house hunt, Sonali gets battle ready when she meets a master armourer and his apprentice who are upholding the tradition of this metalwork craft

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Sisters Liz and Maddie both love to cook and here wish to add some brand-new dishes to their repertoire. Liz is in the Green Pepper kitchen spicing things up with chef Romy Gill and Maddie is in the Red Pepper kitchen bringing out maximum flavour with chef Anna Haugh. Presented by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Antique camera restorer Brenton West tackles a vintage pocket camera, believed to have taken the final photo of a loving family before they dispersed. Amanda Middleditch and Julie Tatchell get to grips with a stuffed pig that is crying out for attention, while ceramics expert Kirsten Ramsay painstakingly pieces back together a delicate stone plate from India

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan host the nightly magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Despite Sharon telling new-found brother Zack to leave, he buys her breakfast - but when her back is turned, he swipes her keys. Kat wants her fling with Phil kept quiet, although after overhearing him booking a romantic holiday, she is secretly overjoyed. Kush visits Ruby about Arthur but she knows he has only plucked up the courage to speak to her because Martin is out. Bailey admits she won the science competition to Mitch, who assures her they'll find the money to attend the camp

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A pair of university graduates hope that their organic superfood powders will supercharge the Dragons' pockets. A low-calorie craft lager business brews up trouble over its high valuation, a dental-minded duo demonstrate their plaque-detecting app, while there's a heartfelt pitch from an entrepreneur hoping to up-scale her range of afro and curly hair products. Whether Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani, Sara Davies or Touker Suleyman will be prompted to invest in all - or any - of the products being pitched remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The final four amateur cooks interpret a classic dish of their choice before the best three face a Chef's Table challenge at Michel Roux Jr's acclaimed restaurant Le Gavroche, As usual, John Torode and Gregg Wallace assess the participants' skills to a bid to assess that they can be worthy finalists, joined in their endeavours by Monica Galetti, Steve Groves and Michel's daughter Emily Roux

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary about the disappearing world of the Belfast Jewish community, as it struggles to gather the 10 men, or Minyan, needed for a prayer service. The film explores why keeping the synagogue open, and the community going, is so important to those who remain. Small Jewish communities are dying all over the UK - this is the story of one of them

programme.title: Avengers Assemble (2012)
programme.desc: A mystical artefact is stolen from a top-secret military base by Thor's brother Loki, who plans to use it to bring about an alien invasion. Spymaster Nick Fury brings together a team of superheroes, including Iron Man, Thor, Captain America and the Hulk, to defend Earth from the impending attack. Action adventure uniting the Marvel comic-book characters, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Evans, Chris Hemsworth, Mark Ruffalo, Scarlett Johansson, Tom Hiddleston, Jeremy Renner and Samuel L Jackson

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of the work of committees set up to investigate aspects of public policy and society

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: HARDtalk: Stephen King Stephen Sackur interviews prolific writer Stephen King. He's written more than 60 books and sold close to 400 million copies. Films like the Shining, Carrie and Stand by Me are all based on his stories. He's been writing horror for half a century, but how has our appetite for fear evolved?

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the Health and Social Care select committee session on the Health and Social Care White Paper with evidence from the Health Secretary, Matt Hancock, and Jason Yiannikkou, Director of NHS Legislation Programme at Department of Health and Social Care

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Prime Minister Boris Johnson

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint National Security Strategy Committee session on the UK's national security machinery, with evidence from former prime minister David Cameron and former national security advisor Peter Rickets, from Monday 1 March.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Former Governor of the Bank of England Mark Carney charts how financial value has come to be held in higher esteem than human value, beginning by assessing what value actually means. How looks at whether the way it is assessed both shapes human values and constrains choices, and looks at how the markets affect society. Introduced by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Despite losing the presidency and both Houses of Congress, Donald Trump still seems to have a chokehold on the Republican party. So what should Republican anti-Trumpers do now? Do they fight from within, or create a new Conservative Party? Stephen Sackur interviews Evan McMullin, one of their key organisers and strategists

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Former prime minister Tony Blair speaking at the Institute for Government on the last twelve months of the Covid-19 pandemic, its lasting impact and the challenges posed for politicians, from Friday 26 February

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: David Cornock tells the story of how Toynbee Hall and London's East End shaped the politics of Labour prime minister Clement Attlee

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the Science and Technology select committee session on UK science, research and technology capability and influence in global disease outbreaks with evidence from the Chief Medical Officer for England, Dr Chris Whitty, and the government's Chief Scientific Adviser, Sir Patrick Vallance

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Science and Technology Committee session on establishing a new UK research funding agency, with evidence from the former adviser to the prime minister Dominic Cummings, and the business secretary Kwasi Kwarteng, from Wednesday 17 March.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Donald Trump's presidency is history. So not surprisingly historians are trying to find some perspective on a president who stress-tested American democracy like no other. Stephen Sackur speaks to Timothy Snyder, one of America's leading historians on totalitarianism and the Holocaust. He believes Trump and his movement brought America face to face with early-stage fascism, but is that pushing historical parallels too far? First shown on the BBC News Channel on 22 February.

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: The debate in the House of Lords on the 2021 Budget, from earlier today.

programme.title: Parliamentary Firsts
programme.desc: Selina Seth presents a short guide to the history of diversity in the House of Commons

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Three former chancellors, Norman Lamont, Alistair Darling and George Osborne, in conversation on 'tax in the real world' at the Institute for Government, from Tuesday 9 March

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the Welsh Assembly as First Minister Mark Drakeford tackles queries on issues affecting life in Wales

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his second Reith Lecture, Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, takes his audience back to the high drama of the financial crisis of 2008, which ended a period when bankers saw themselves as unassailable Masters of the Universe. More than a decade on, how much have the bankers changed their ways? How far has the financial sector changed? Dr Carney says that society must remain vigilant and resist the 'three lies of finance'. If not, it will live with a system that is ill-prepared for the next crisis. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint Lords and Commons committee session examining the UK government's review of the Human Rights Act 1998, with evidence from the former president of the UK supreme court Baroness Hale, from Wednesday 3 February

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to American psychologist professor Laurie Santos, whose work on the science of happiness has won her an audience of millions far beyond the lecture theatre. With Covid putting enormous pressure on mental health, can we really learn how to be happy?

programme.title: Stormont: From Parliament to Power Sharing
programme.desc: BBC Parliament's Faye Kidd presents a brief history of the Northern Ireland legislature from Parliament to power-sharing

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate Coverage of the International Women's Day debate in the House of Commons, from Thursday 11 March.

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate Coverage of the International Women's Day debate in the House of Commons, from Thursday 11 March.

programme.title: Ten Minute Rule Bill
programme.desc: SNP MP Douglas Chapman introduces his Bill to the House of Commons under the ten minute rule, covering the automated purchase and resale of gaming hardware to tackle the problem of online 'scalping' relating to games consoles, from Wednesday 24 March.

programme.title: The Treaty of Versailles
programme.desc: Professor Margaret MacMillan delivers a Gresham College lecture looking back at the Treaty of Versailles a hundred years later, from 4 June 2019

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: Conservative party historian Lord Lexden charts the career of Andrew Bonar Law from his birth in Canada to Downing Street, via Pembroke Lodge in Kensington

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Home Affairs Committee session on the work of the UK government's Home Office, with evidence from the home secretary Priti Patel and officials, from Wednesday 24 February

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the Booker prize-winning author Douglas Stuart. His novel, Shuggie Bain, centres on a boy growing up amid poverty, addiction and intolerance in Glasgow. There are deep parallels with his own life. How does he extract so much love from hardship? First shown on the BBC News Channel on 18 February.

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Statement in the Assembly on updates to the Covid-19 response and social restrictions in Northern Ireland, with first minister Arlene Foster, from Tuesday 16 March

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Treasury select committee session taking evidence on the 2021 Budget from the Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rishi Sunak

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, observes that the pandemic has forced states to confront how people value health, wealth and opportunity. During the first few months of the crisis, most states chose to value human life more than the economic well-being of the nation-state. But if that seems to be changing how do we assess value in this sense? Dr Carney elucidates surprising differences in the financial value put on a human life in different nations - and goes on to argue that this reductionist approach fails to take into account deeper thinking about the worth of human existence. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The idea of a social contract between the individual and the state is a staple of political philosophy. But what happens when that contract is threatened by forces beyond the control of any government, like a climate crisis or, right now, a global pandemic? Stephen Sackur speaks to Minouche Shafik, director of the London School of Economics and former top official at the World Bank. Is humanity capable of collective action to meet global challenges?

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of a session of the DCMS Committee on the economics of music streaming, with witnesses from Amazon, Apple and Spotify, as well as DCMS minister Caroline Dinenage, from Tuesday 23 February. First shown on BBC Parliament on 27 February.

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Recorded coverage

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: MPs on the Health and Social Care Committee hear from NHS England Chief Executive Sir Simon Stevens and NHS England Chief Operating Officer Amanda Pritchard, from Tuesday 9 March

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his final lecture, Mark Carney turns his attention to climate change, arguing that the roots of our environmental emergency lie in a deeper crisis of values

programme.desc: Recorded coverage of the work of committees set up to investigate aspects of public policy and society

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Human rights activist Azimjan Askarov was imprisoned in Kyrgyzstan in 2010 for a crime he says he did not commit. Ever since then, his wife Hadicha has campaigned tirelessly for his release. Now she sees one last chance for justice, in an appeal hearing at the country's supreme court. But as Hadicha prepares for the verdict, coronavirus is spreading across Kyrgyzstan, stopping Hadicha's prison visits and putting 69-year-old Azimjan at risk. Our World follows an extraordinary story of love, courage - and never giving up.

programme.title: I Can't Say My Name: Stammering in the Spotlight
programme.desc: BBC producer Felicity Baker has a stammer that she has spent her whole life trying to hide. She struggles to say her own name. Now she is stepping out in front of the camera for the first time to talk about what it is like to live with the condition - and she soon finds she is not alone. She meets a rugby player who blames his stammer for his aggressive behaviour on the pitch, a rapper who found music stopped him stammering and the comedy star Michael Palin, whose father stammered but never ever spoke about it

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Human rights activist Azimjan Askarov was imprisoned in Kyrgyzstan in 2010 for a crime he says he did not commit. Ever since then, his wife Hadicha has campaigned tirelessly for his release. Now she sees one last chance for justice, in an appeal hearing at the country's supreme court. But as Hadicha prepares for the verdict, coronavirus is spreading across Kyrgyzstan, stopping Hadicha's prison visits and putting 69-year-old Azimjan at risk. Our World follows an extraordinary story of love, courage - and never giving up.

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bob is worried that Dan is flirting with Wendy, Charity wants to make peace with her children, and Andrea asks Charles out for a drink

programme.title: For the Love of Britain
programme.desc: Julie Walters presents a guide to some of the most beautiful sights Sussex and Kent have to offer. Actor Robert Bathurst heads on a walk through Rye to Camber Sands, his home for more than 20 years, and Zoe Ball visits Standen House to check out one of the country's most celebrated gardens. The iconic white cliffs of Dover wow Liz Bonnin with a great British view, while in Whitstable, Ade Edmondson tastes the local delicacy, oysters

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz, in which another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck

programme.title: Britain's Tiger Kings - On the Trail with Ross Kemp
programme.desc: Part two of two. The presenter meets people who own dangerous and exotic animals in Britain, including a man who keeps 46 snakes and a crocodile owner with an unusual death wish. Later, he discovers what happens when wild cats escape

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Station: Trouble on the Tracks
programme.desc: Documentary following the staff of Network Rail, which operates the country's railway tracks, focusing on Birmingham New Street station. Engineers battle the elements after extensive flooding, while beleaguered staff deal with stranded passengers demanding answers. Graffiti artist Banksy leaves Birmingham rail engineers a gift spray-painted on to one of the walls - all done under his usual veil of secrecy - and train manager Adam is faced with a passenger without a ticket

programme.title: The Metro: A Rail Life Story
programme.desc: Documentary following workers on the Tyne and Wear Metro - the UK's largest metro system outside of London. Staff in Sunderland prepare for the arrival of 50,000 Spice Girls fans ahead of the pop group's concert at the Stadium of Light. The pressure rises across the day as head of operations Wayne spots a potential fault with an overhead line, and there's an anxious call to the control room - a set of fans have lost their tickets for the concert of a lifetime. Narrated by Zoe Hakin

programme.desc: A decade on from the momentous occasion, this special programme looks back at the wedding of Prince William and Kate Middleton in April 2011. The documentary also casts a fresh eye over the couple's courtship, looking at how they are modernising the monarchy, and how they are preparing for their future roles as Britain's King and Queen

programme.title: My Years with the Queen
programme.desc: Pamela Hicks, the daughter of Louis Mountbatten, talks about her life growing up within the Royal family, and her close relationship with the Queen. Pamela will also be talking to her daughter India, who was a bridesmaid at the wedding of Prince Charles and Diana Spencer. With the aid of rarely seen photos and extracts from Pamela's personal diaries, this film offers a personal perspective of the Queen told by someone who was there with her at key moments during her reign

programme.title: The Queen Unseen
programme.desc: The Queen's coronation made her the most famous woman in the world. But her anointment, the moment she became Queen Elizabeth II, was so sacred it was hidden and not allowed to be filmed. In this documentary, a clinical psychologist unpicks her guarded body language, and lip-readers breathe new life into iconic Coronation balcony archive

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: John Bishop's Great Whale Rescue
programme.desc: Part two of two. John's mission to return Little White and Little Grey from captivity to the Icelandic beluga whale sanctuary continues, as the pair begin the 6,000-mile journey to reach their new home. Following the previous delays in China, the release is already two months behind schedule, and as autumn approaches the whales have little time to put on enough weight and acclimatise to the cold water they will face in the bay

programme.title: The Station: Trouble on the Tracks
programme.desc: Documentary following the staff of Network Rail, which operates the country's railway tracks, focusing on Birmingham New Street station. Engineers battle the elements after extensive flooding, while beleaguered staff deal with stranded passengers demanding answers. Graffiti artist Banksy leaves Birmingham rail engineers a gift spray-painted on to one of the walls - all done under his usual veil of secrecy - and train manager Adam is faced with a passenger without a ticket

programme.title: The Metro: A Rail Life Story
programme.desc: Documentary following workers on the Tyne and Wear Metro - the UK's largest metro system outside of London. Staff in Sunderland prepare for the arrival of 50,000 Spice Girls fans ahead of the pop group's concert at the Stadium of Light. The pressure rises across the day as head of operations Wayne spots a potential fault with an overhead line, and there's an anxious call to the control room - a set of fans have lost their tickets for the concert of a lifetime. Narrated by Zoe Hakin

programme.title: My Years with the Queen
programme.desc: Pamela Hicks, the daughter of Louis Mountbatten, talks about her life growing up within the Royal family, and her close relationship with the Queen. programme.title: John Bishop's Great Whale Rescue
programme.desc: Part two of two. John's mission to return Little White and Little Grey from captivity to the Icelandic beluga whale sanctuary continues, as the pair begin the 6,000-mile journey to reach their new home. Following the previous delays in China, the release is already two months behind schedule, and as autumn approaches the whales have little time to put on enough weight and acclimatise to the cold water they will face in the bay

programme.desc: Comedy routines by Glenn Moore, Adam Hess, Dane Baptiste, Suzi Rufell, Seann Walsh, Catherine Bohart, Alex Edelman and Brennan Reece are reconstructed as sketches

programme.title: The Stand Up Sketch Show
programme.desc: Routines by comedians including Stephen Bailey, Tom Allen, Felicity Ward and Joel Dommett are reconstructed as sketches

programme.title: Don't Hate the Playaz
programme.desc: Maya Jama, Mutya Buena and Aurie Styla go head to head in rap battles against Lady Leshurr, Yung Filly and Chunkz, with a special appearance from Dizzee Rascal

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Iain Stirling hosts as five friends take on Scarlett Moffatt, Samira Mighty, Tom Parker, London Hughes and Brennan Reece in challenges based on unusual abilities

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: More Incredible and peculiar record-breaking attempts, featuring Australian diving pigs, the most tattooed man on the planet and dogs removing socks from people's feet. Matt Edmonds narrates

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Donna and Kelly try to find Andy in Leeds. Zak hatches a plan to get the church money back. Scott tries to convince Roy that Kelly isn't the right girl for him

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon schemes to get Biff to admit he fancies Kathy. Zak, Butch and Mandy attempt to dupe some American tourists. Chris discovers more of Reynolds' underhand tactics

programme.title: Carry On Girls (1973)
programme.desc: The council of a seaside town is in dire need of a means to attract tourists to its resort, so announces a beauty contest. While the conniving organiser plots a series of publicity stunts, an outraged feminist group plans to sabotage the event. Comedy, starring Sid James, June Whitfield, Joan Sims, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Bresslaw and Peter Butterworth

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sally and Mike bring home a tramp, much to Sid's disgust. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Suzy gives Stan food from the canteen to take home - but it's stolen property. When Blakey discovers that Stan and Jack are biting the hand that feeds them, he vows to make trouble. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd as the Cornish copper. When a bookshop owner is murdered in Penzance, the detective discovers all is not harmonious between the members of one of the area's most prominent families. With Kevin Quarmby and Carla Mendonca

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Kate seeks Nick's advice about one of her patients, who seems to be receiving threats, and Greengrass tries to take the law into his own hands. Dora Bryan guest stars

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy invites Tricia to the Vet's Ball. Andy is allowed to stay with the Sugdens, who plan to adopt him. Zak gives Ashley the stolen money back. Roy pesters Kelly to marry him

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Donna worries about Viv's reaction to her news. Marlon schemes to get Biff and Kathy together. Scott almost blurts out Kelly's secret to Roy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma is shocked to hear Roy tell her that he is seeing Hayley. Gail is horrified when Nick moves his things out. Maxine feels awful for getting Fiona into trouble

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne and Nick enjoy the party Les and Janice throw for them. Des confronts Ritchie in London. Roy and Hayley find themselves stuck in a tunnel at the protest

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Geraldine McEwan makes her last appearance as the spinster sleuth before handing over the knitting needles to Julia McKenzie. Following the death of old acquaintance Mr Rafiel, Miss Marple receives instructions drawn up prior to his demise asking her to embark on a coach tour to investigate an unspecified possible crime. It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. Murder mystery, with a star-studded supporting cast including Richard E Grant, Johnny Briggs, Amanda Burton, Anne Reid, Ronni Ancona, George Cole, Will Mellor and Laura Michelle Kelly

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian detective, who usually only accepts cases of national importance, bends his rules for once and finds himself in run-down Clapham. He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The mysterious shooting of archaeology student Celia Dawe and the significance of matching playing cards lead Wycliffe on a manhunt across Cornwall. Police drama based on the novel by WJ Burley, guest starring Bill Nighy alongside Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin are called to investigate when a man is attacked and his kidney is surgically removed. The focus quickly turns to finding the intended recipient of the organ, and the team is horrified to discover that it has been trafficked into the NHS and given to wealthy patient Joanna Woodleigh. When the case is handed over to the CPS, a conflict of interests becomes apparent as Steel and Phillips go up against George Castle in court. Denis Lawson guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Kate: The Making of a Modern Queen
programme.desc: Profile of Kate Middleton, how she has changed as person since her marriage to Prince William and what she means for the royal family. From comparatively modest circumstances, the Middleton family could not be more different from the Windsors, yet this is exactly what Prince William wanted - a royal life combined with comfortable marital stability and a continuation of the easier relationship with the British people started by his mother, Princess Diana. The couple met at St Andrew's University in 2001 and after several ups and downs were married at Westminster Abbey in 2011. Contributors include Anthony Seldon, Ferdinand Mount, Catherine Mayer, Emily Nash, Katie Nichol and Sarah Bradford

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The detective discovers his half-sister's daughter has committed suicide and sets out to find what prompted it, entering the world of teenage rave parties and drug-taking. Meanwhile, Lewis has his sights set on promotion and studies for exams. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Sorcha Cusack, Jason Isaacs and John Junkin

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack feel guilty about an accident in the bus, so they volunteer to care for the Inspector's pets while he is on holiday. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A flu epidemic hits the depot, improving Stan's financial health as he and Jack clock up overtime. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian detective, who usually only accepts cases of national importance, bends his rules for once and finds himself in run-down Clapham. He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The mysterious shooting of archaeology student Celia Dawe and the significance of matching playing cards lead Wycliffe on a manhunt across Cornwall. Police drama based on the novel by WJ Burley, guest starring Bill Nighy alongside Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick is left with a sore head following an incident in a graveyard, and Blaketon experiences trouble at home. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice tells Mandy to scrub the cellar while she and Tricia go to the ball. Zoe rescues Mandy from the cellar and invites her to the celebrations

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samantha apologises to Des for lying to him. Roy and Hayley decide to become more involved in the protest. Ashley criticises Zoe for neglecting Shannon

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The bulldozers move in on the Rec. Roy asks Professor Barker to look at the Roman relics and declares them genuine. Fiona goes into premature labour

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of an amateur flautist and the disappearance of a maid on a secluded country estate soon have Detective Superintendent Charles Wycliffe unravelling a mystery involving family feuds and illicit relationships. Police drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As plans to build a new police station lead Morse to question his place in the force, he investigates the case of Tommy Cork, a boy from a broken home who has disappeared. Meanwhile, the body of disreputable journalist Eric Patterson has been found on railway tracks in mysterious circumstances. He was last seen embroiled in an argument with a council official about a company preparing to redevelop the site of Blenheim Vale, a disused correctional facility for boys. When a petty criminal with a connection to Blenheim absconds from an open prison, it sets off a chain reaction of events that threaten to expose horrors of the past. Did missing Tommy witness something that cost him his life?

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate the death of middle-class teenager Debbie Powell. They discover the victim had been living a secret life as a drugs mule, and was being led astray by local dealer Gerry Craig. The case has an emotional impact on Castle, who demands Steel and Phillips do everything in their power to bring the person responsible to justice. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Bill Paterson star

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Unexplained killings rock the Bodmin community, giving rise to fears that the so-called 'beast', a monster enshrined in local legend, has returned to wreak fresh havoc. Kersey ventures up onto the moors to investigate and does not return - so Wycliffe is forced to go it alone. Jack Shepherd and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A former bookie falls under suspicion for the murder of a hospital benefactor, who is found dead in his car. As the detectives investigate the case, it becomes clear their prime suspect had a strong motive, believing the victim was responsible for putting his daughter into a coma. Brian Cox guest stars, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The couple go to a family wedding, but they don't bargain on Jean's cousin Peggy being there

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The new depot nurse moves in as the Butlers' lodger - causing problems for Arthur. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney and Michael Robbins

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A woman arrives at the depot to claim her lost property, but Stan and Jack have already eaten it. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of an amateur flautist and the disappearance of a maid on a secluded country estate soon have Detective Superintendent Charles Wycliffe unravelling a mystery involving family feuds and illicit relationships. Police drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A hit-and-run incident alerts Nick to the presence of badger-baiters in the village, but the outsiders from Middlesbrough will stop at nothing in pursuit of their 'sport'. Meanwhile, Greengrass finds a way of making racehorse ownership pay

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy is over the moon that Mandy has accepted his proposal. Chris is convinced Sean Reynolds is behind the break-in. Scott takes the news of Kelly's engagement badly

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Viv is happy about Roy and Kelly and offers to pay for the whole wedding. Mandy asks for advice about annulling her marriage to Butch. Chris breaks into Sean Reynolds' office

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona celebrates the birth of her son. Ashley does not like the idea of Zoe living off the State. Natalie tells Des that Samantha really loves him

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Toyah and Emily are stunned when Spider tells them he is moving on to a protest in Kent. Gail does not like the idea of Nick sharing a house with Zoe

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: An ancient Cornish folk ritual ends in the murder of a local undertaker - and provides quick-witted copper Charles Wycliffe with one of the most baffling mysteries of his career. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters, Jimmy Yuill and Susan Penhaligon star

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway appears to have got over his fear of Lewis treading over his newly promoted toes. Their opposites have attracted in the past, and there seems no reason to suspect that they won't now the younger man has got used to the idea of his former mentor coming out of retirement to lend him a hand. Even DS Maddox isn't getting on Hathaway's nerves quite as much, so this rejuvenated trio sets out to discover who murdered American classics student Rose Anderson, whose body has been hauled from the canal with neck and abdomen wounds. Nevertheless, a few red herrings threaten to get in their way of landing the killer. Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox and Angela Griffin star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks agrees to help old friend and partner Jimmy Valentine with an undercover drug sting, but when the bust goes wrong and a dealer ends up dead, suspicion falls on the officers. As Devlin pushes for the truth, the spotlight turns on Brooks' dark past, and a 10-year-old case involving no witnesses and a large quantity of missing heroin. Guest starring Hustle's Robert Glenister, former Rising Damp star Don Warrington and Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral), alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Suspicion falls on a pagan sect steeped in ancient beliefs when a baby is found dead. As the detective investigates, he has to juggle family and work commitments, and finds that friendship and professionalism are pushed to the limit. Jack Shepherd stars, with Jimmy Yuill and Helen Masters

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An opera singer is shot by a sniper at an Oxford University ceremony just as she is about to receive an honorary degree. Morse is a big fan of her work and thinks she could not possibly be involved in anything untoward, but his investigations turn up more than a few sordid secrets. Detective drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Robert Hardy, Sheila Gish, John Gielgud and Rachel Weisz

He was last seen embroiled in an argument with a council official about a company preparing to redevelop the site of Blenheim Vale, a disused correctional facility for boys. When a petty criminal with a connection to Blenheim absconds from an open prison, it sets off a chain reaction of events that threaten to expose horrors of the past. Did missing Tommy witness something that cost him his life?

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate the death of middle-class teenager Debbie Powell. They discover the victim had been living a secret life as a drugs mule, and was being led astray by local dealer Gerry Craig. The case has an emotional impact on Castle, who demands Steel and Phillips do everything in their power to bring the person responsible to justice. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Bill Paterson star

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Unexplained killings rock the Bodmin community, giving rise to fears that the so-called 'beast', a monster enshrined in local legend, has returned to wreak fresh havoc. Kersey ventures up onto the moors to investigate and does not return - so Wycliffe is forced to go it alone. Jack Shepherd and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A former bookie falls under suspicion for the murder of a hospital benefactor, who is found dead in his car. As the detectives investigate the case, it becomes clear their prime suspect had a strong motive, believing the victim was responsible for putting his daughter into a coma. Brian Cox guest stars, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Samantha apologises to Des for lying to him. Roy and Hayley decide to become more involved in the protest. Ashley criticises Zoe for neglecting Shannon

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The bulldozers move in on the Rec. Roy asks Professor Barker to look at the Roman relics and declares them genuine. Fiona goes into premature labour

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice tells Mandy to scrub the cellar while she and Tricia go to the ball. Zoe rescues Mandy from the cellar and invites her to the celebrations

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick is left with a sore head following an incident in a graveyard, and Blaketon experiences trouble at home. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The couple go to a family wedding, but they don't bargain on Jean's cousin Peggy being there

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The new depot nurse moves in as the Butlers' lodger - causing problems for Arthur. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney and Michael Robbins

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A woman arrives at the depot to claim her lost property, but Stan and Jack have already eaten it. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of an amateur flautist and the disappearance of a maid on a secluded country estate soon have Detective Superintendent Charles Wycliffe unravelling a mystery involving family feuds and illicit relationships. Police drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A hit-and-run incident alerts Nick to the presence of badger-baiters in the village, but the outsiders from Middlesbrough will stop at nothing in pursuit of their 'sport'. Meanwhile, Greengrass finds a way of making racehorse ownership pay

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy is over the moon that Mandy has accepted his proposal. Chris is convinced Sean Reynolds is behind the break-in. Scott takes the news of Kelly's engagement badly

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Viv is happy about Roy and Kelly and offers to pay for the whole wedding. Mandy asks for advice about annulling her marriage to Butch. Chris breaks into Sean Reynolds' office

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona celebrates the birth of her son. Ashley does not like the idea of Zoe living off the State. Natalie tells Des that Samantha really loves him

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Toyah and Emily are stunned when Spider tells them he is moving on to a protest in Kent. Gail does not like the idea of Nick sharing a house with Zoe

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: An ancient Cornish folk ritual ends in the murder of a local undertaker - and provides quick-witted copper Charles Wycliffe with one of the most baffling mysteries of his career. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters, Jimmy Yuill and Susan Penhaligon star

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway appears to have got over his fear of Lewis treading over his newly promoted toes. Their opposites have attracted in the past, and there seems no reason to suspect that they won't now the younger man has got used to the idea of his former mentor coming out of retirement to lend him a hand. Even DS Maddox isn't getting on Hathaway's nerves quite as much, so this rejuvenated trio sets out to discover who murdered American classics student Rose Anderson, whose body has been hauled from the canal with neck and abdomen wounds. Nevertheless, a few red herrings threaten to get in their way of landing the killer. Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox and Angela Griffin star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks agrees to help old friend and partner Jimmy Valentine with an undercover drug sting, but when the bust goes wrong and a dealer ends up dead, suspicion falls on the officers. As Devlin pushes for the truth, the spotlight turns on Brooks' dark past, and a 10-year-old case involving no witnesses and a large quantity of missing heroin. Guest starring Hustle's Robert Glenister, former Rising Damp star Don Warrington and Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral), alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Suspicion falls on a pagan sect steeped in ancient beliefs when a baby is found dead. As the detective investigates, he has to juggle family and work commitments, and finds that friendship and professionalism are pushed to the limit. Jack Shepherd stars, with Jimmy Yuill and Helen Masters

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An opera singer is shot by a sniper at an Oxford University ceremony just as she is about to receive an honorary degree. Morse is a big fan of her work and thinks she could not possibly be involved in anything untoward, but his investigations turn up more than a few sordid secrets. Detective drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Robert Hardy, Sheila Gish, John Gielgud and Rachel Weisz

programme.desc: Arthur is captivated by an attractive female vocalist, who boasts limited musical talent. programme.desc: Regan's informer Popeye is abducted after passing on information about a switch of some stolen diamonds. Working with Carter, the DI must catch the villains while protecting their source. Roy Kinnear guest stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Terminal 4 turns blue, airside officer Hugh has a potential environmental mess on his hands, and an octogenarian forces herself into the country under the watchful eye of Passenger Experience Guru Demi. Narrated by Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. programme.title: Robin Hood (2010)

Ridley Scott's medieval adventure, starring Russell Crowe, Cate Blanchett and Mark Strong

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, married couple Paul and Debbie kick off the competition with a menu of tried and tested dinner party favourites. However, engaged couple Angela and John aren't impressed by their simple fare and posh couple Kirsty and James find Paul's behaviour even less palatable. Perfectionists Angela and John cook up dishes inspired by their favourite restaurant meals. Finally, Kirsty and James plan an organic, locally sourced menu. At the table there's a lesson in asparagus eating and the hosts get a dose of their own medicine when their guests return their food criticism

programme.title: 50 Years of the Troubles: A Journey Through Film
programme.desc: Documentary marking the half century since sectarian riots erupted across Northern Ireland, widely seen as the beginning of the 30-year conflict known as The Troubles. Film maker Mark Cousins, who left Belfast at 18, returns to his home town to reflect on how the place and its history have been used, and occasionally abused, by cinema

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Families from opposite ends of the wealth and class divide swap homes, budgets and social status for seven days to discover how the other half lives. This week, the Caddy family of Bristol trade places with the Williams family from Weston-super-Mare. The Caddys rank in the top 10 per cent of Britain's wealthy, and live in a seven-bedroom home, while for the eight-strong Williams family, who are among the nation's poorest 10 per cent, the swap means leaving their rented three-bedroom house and enjoying a weekly budget of £1,700 instead of their usual £110

programme.title: The Vanishing of Suzy Lamplugh
programme.desc: Examining the case surrounding Suzy Lamplugh's disappearance in 1986, tracing the dramatic twists and turns of the case across more than three decades. The programme explores what went wrong with the original investigation, and also draws upon the police's reinvestigation of the case, and follows the emergence of a prime suspect in Suzy's murder

programme.title: Countdown to Murder
programme.desc: Documentary examining some of Britain's most notorious murders, charting the last few days leading up to the killing from the perspectives of both perpetrator and victim, using dramatic reconstruction to pinpoint the trigger that set each one in motion, providing in-depth psychological analysis and scrutiny of the devious and calculating plans the killers formulated. This episode examines the events leading up to the death of Natalie Jarvis in 2012, who was murdered by Adam Whelehan, a man with whom she had been having a casual relationship

programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell offers an insight into the history, design and building process of the Britannia Bridge, across the Menai Strait between the island of Anglesey and the mainland of Wales. The difficult task of building the 100ft iron structure was taken up by civil engineer Robert Stephenson and his team, including William Fairbairn and Eaton Hodgkinson. But in May 1970, four youths set light to the famous bridge, and Rob meets a fireman who fought the blaze that fateful night

The programme explores how new police restrictions are impacting on the festival's traditions, as well as the disruption caused by torrential rain, including a nearby river rising to a dangerous level.
